17	// Env-variable helpers for Helm-driven configuration (Phase 3 plan 03-09).
18	//
19	// These helpers exist as testable units (cmd/manager/env_test.go) because
20	// the main() function itself is not unit-testable (it constructs the
21	// controller-runtime Manager and blocks on mgr.Start). Helpers cover the
22	// per-level model defaults (D-C4) and leader-election tuning (D-D1).
23	package main
24	
25	import (
26		"os"
27		"strconv"
28		"time"
29	
30		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/controller"
31	)
32	
33	// envOrDefault returns the value of the env var named key, or fallback when
34	// the env var is unset or empty. Empty-string is treated as unset so a Helm
35	// value left at its zero default (e.g. `value: ""`) cleanly falls through to
36	// the binary's compile-time default rather than overriding it with "".
37	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
38		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
39			return v
40		}
41		return fallback
42	}
43	
44	// atoiOrDefault returns the integer value of the env var named key, or
45	// fallback when the env var is unset, empty, or not an integer.
46	// Non-integer values are tolerated (return fallback) so a stray non-numeric
47	// value in the Helm chart cannot crash-loop the controller.
48	func atoiOrDefault(key string, fallback int) int {
49		v := os.Getenv(key)
50		if v == "" {
51			return fallback
52		}
53		n, err := strconv.Atoi(v)
54		if err != nil {
55			return fallback
56		}
57		return n
58	}
59	
60	// resolvePerLevelModels returns the four-key map from level name
61	// ("milestone"|"phase"|"plan"|"task") to the model identifier used as the
62	// last-fallback in dispatch_helpers.ResolveProvider (D-C2).
63	//
64	// Defaults match D-C4 — phase brief lock-in:
65	//
66	//	milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
67	//	phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
68	//	plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
69	//	task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
70	//
71	// Override per-level via env vars (set by the manager Deployment from Helm
72	// values.yaml subagent.levels.{level}.model):
73	//
74	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
75	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
76	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
77	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
78	func resolvePerLevelModels() map[string]string {
79		return map[string]string{
80			"milestone": envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE", "claude-opus-4-7"),
81			"phase":     envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
82			"plan":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
83			"task":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK", "claude-haiku-4-5"),
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// tideHelmProviderDefaults builds the controller.ProviderDefaults struct
88	// (D-C2 last-fallback in ResolveProvider) from the Helm-chart subagent image
89	// + per-level model map. claudeImage is the default subagent image
90	// (CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var); per-level models come from
91	// resolvePerLevelModels (TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_* env vars).
92	//
93	// Called once at Manager startup; the result is stamped onto each up-stack
94	// reconciler's HelmProviderDefaults field (MilestoneReconciler /
95	// PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler).
96	func tideHelmProviderDefaults(claudeImage string) controller.ProviderDefaults {
97		return controller.ProviderDefaults{
98			Image:  claudeImage,
99			Models: resolvePerLevelModels(),
100		}
101	}
102	
103	// resolveLeaderElectionTiming returns the (lease, renew, retry) durations
104	// for controller-runtime's LeaderElection block (D-D1 / Phase 3 chaos-resume).
105	//
106	// Defaults pin to common controller-runtime production values
107	// (lease=15s, renew=10s, retry=2s) which give a single-pod restart ≤25s
108	// failover ceiling, with the invariant lease > renew > retry preserved.
109	// Override via env vars (set by manager Deployment from Helm values.yaml
110	// leaderElection.*):
111	//
112	//	TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS  (default 15)
113	//	TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS  (default 10)
114	//	TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS  (default 2)
115	func resolveLeaderElectionTiming() (lease, renew, retry time.Duration) {
116		lease = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS", 15)) * time.Second
117		renew = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS", 10)) * time.Second
118		retry = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS", 2)) * time.Second
119		return lease, renew, retry
120	}

# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
#
# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
#
# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
# Phase 5-compatible foundation.

# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
controllerManager:
  manager:
    args:
    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
    - --leader-elect
    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
    containerSecurityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    env:
      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
      watchNamespace: ""
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
      tag: ""
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    podAnnotations: {}
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 128Mi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 64Mi
  podSecurityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccount:
    annotations: {}
kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
metricsService:
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8443
  type: ClusterIP
webhookService:
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 9443
  type: ClusterIP

# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
#
# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
plannerConcurrency: 16
executorConcurrency: 4

# In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest — tag-based pulls of mutable tags
  # are not reproducible across pulls.
  claudeSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 09 plan 09-06 (REQ-09-01): in-namespace reporter image. The reader Job
  # runs in the project namespace as tide-reporter SA, reads out.json from the PVC,
  # and materializes child CRDs via the K8s API (Option C architecture).
  # Wired to controller via TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
  # When empty, the spawn site skips Job creation (same as TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE skip in
  # boundary_push.go). In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest.
  tideReporter:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-reporter
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C4): per-level subagent defaults. Resolution chain at
# dispatch time (in MilestoneReconciler / PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler before
# calling Dispatcher.Run):
#
#   Levels.<level>.Image (project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.image) ← per-level override
#   → Spec.Subagent.Image (project.Spec.subagent.image)               ← per-Project default
#   → subagent.defaults.image (this value → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env) ← Helm chart default
#
#   project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.model  ← per-level model override
#   project.Spec.subagent.model                 ← per-Project model default
#   subagent.defaults.model                     ← Helm chart default (this block)
#   subagent.levels.<level>.model               ← Helm per-level default (this block)
#
# Phase 13 D-01 image posture: production default is the real Claude-backed subagent
# (ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent). The stub is explicit opt-in for tests/CI
# only — it is never injected implicitly. To use the stub:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
#
# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
#
# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
subagent:
  defaults:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  levels:
    milestone:
      model: claude-opus-4-7
    phase:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    plan:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    task:
      model: claude-haiku-4-5

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
gitleaks:
  configMapName: ""

# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding

1	# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
2	# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
3	# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
4	#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
5	#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
6	#
7	# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
8	FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
9	ARG TARGETOS
10	ARG TARGETARCH
11	
12	WORKDIR /workspace
13	
14	# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
15	COPY go.mod go.sum ./
16	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download
17	
18	# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
19	COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
20	COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
21	COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
22	COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
23	COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/
24	
25	# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
26	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
27	    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
28	      -ldflags="-s -w" \
29	      -o /out/claude-subagent \
30	      ./cmd/claude-subagent
31	
32	# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
33	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
34	# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
35	# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
36	# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
37	# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
38	# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
39	#
40	# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
41	# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
42	# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
43	# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
44	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
45	# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
46	FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732
47	
48	RUN apt-get update \
49	    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
50	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
51	    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
52	    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
53	    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
54	    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
55	    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
56	    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
57	    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'
58	
59	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142
60	
61	COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent
62	
63	# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
64	USER 1000
65	
66	ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
USER 1000

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Working Rules
6	
7	Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.
8	
9	### 1. Observe First
10	
11	Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.
12	
13	1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
14	2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
15	3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
16	4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
17	5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
18	6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**
19	
20	Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.
21	
22	### 2. Execute, Don't Ask
23	
24	When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.
25	
26	**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**
27	
28	- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
29	- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
30	- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
31	- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
32	- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
33	- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
34	- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.
35	
36	### 3. Verify Before Claiming
37	
38	Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.
39	
40	Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:
41	
42	- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
43	- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
44	- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
45	- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
46	- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
47	- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
48	- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
49	- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)
50	
51	## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)
52	
53	Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.
54	
55	- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
56	- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
57	  ```
58	  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
59	  ```
60	  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Working Rules

Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.

### 1. Observe First

Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.

1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:

- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)

## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)

Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.

- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
  ```
  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
  ```
  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "tide.fullname" . }}-controller-manager
  labels:
    control-plane: controller-manager
  {{- include "tide.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.controllerManager.replicas }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
      control-plane: controller-manager
    {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
        control-plane: controller-manager
      {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
      annotations:
        kubectl.kubernetes.io/default-container: manager
        {{- with .Values.controllerManager.manager.podAnnotations }}
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
        {{- end }}
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
          {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.args | nindent 10 }}
          - --credproxy-image={{ .Values.images.credProxy.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.credProxy.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
          - --default-file-touch-mode={{ .Values.planAdmission.fileTouchMode | default "warn" }}
